Observable Plot Examples

All charts use Observable Plot directly with plain JSON data. Swap in any JSON array of objects.

Bar Line / Area Dot / Scatter Pie / Donut Histogram Rect Treemap / Circle Pack Text / Tick / Tip Multi-Y / Faceted Canada Map

Bar Charts

barX — horizontal bars

Plot.barX(data, { x: "value", y: "category", fill: "category" }).plot()

barY — vertical bars

Plot.barY(data, { x: "category", y: "value", fill: "category" }).plot()

groupedBar — bars grouped by series

Plot.barY(data, Plot.groupX({ y: "sum" }, { x: "category", y: "value", fill: "series" })).plot()

percentageBarY — stacked 100% bar

Plot.barY(data, Plot.stackY(Plot.normalizeY("sum"), { x: "category", y: "value", fill: "series" })).plot()

Line / Area

line

Plot.lineY(data, { x: "date", y: "value", stroke: "series" }).plot()

area

Plot.areaY(data, { x: "date", y: "value", fill: "series", fillOpacity: 0.4 }).plot()

Dot / Scatter

dot

Plot.dot(data, { x: "x", y: "y", fill: "series", r: "size", opacity: 0.6 }).plot()

Pie / Donut

pie

// Observable Plot uses arc marks for pie charts
Plot.plot({
  marks: [Plot.arc(data, { theta: "value", fill: "category" })],
  width: 300, height: 300
})

donut

Plot.plot({ marks: [Plot.arc(data, { theta: "value", fill: "category", innerRadius: 60 })], width: 300, height: 300 })

Histogram

histogram (binned)

Plot.rectY(data, Plot.binX({ y: "count" }, { x: "value" })).plot()

histogramX (horizontal)

Plot.rectX(data, Plot.binY({ x: "count" }, { y: "value" })).plot()

Rect

rectY

Plot.rectY(data, { x1: "x1", x2: "x2", y: "value", fill: "category" }).plot()

rectX

Plot.rectX(data, { y1: "y1", y2: "y2", x: "value", fill: "category" }).plot()

rectY faceted

Plot.rectY(data, Plot.binX({ y: "count" }, { x: "value", fx: "category" })).plot()

Treemap / Circle Pack

treemap

// Treemap uses d3-hierarchy — build the hierarchy then render rects
Plot.plot({
  marks: [
    Plot.rect(leaves, { x1: d => d.x0, x2: d => d.x1, y1: d => d.y0, y2: d => d.y1, fill: d => d.data.category }),
    Plot.text(leaves, { x: d => (d.x0+d.x1)/2, y: d => (d.y0+d.y1)/2, text: d => d.data.label })
  ]
})

circlePack

// Circle pack uses d3.pack() — render as dots
Plot.dot(nodes, { x: d => d.x, y: d => d.y, r: d => d.r, fill: d => d.data.category, opacity: 0.7 }).plot()

Text / Tick / Tip

text labels on a chart

Plot.plot({
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(data, { x: "category", y: "value", fill: "steelblue" }),
    Plot.text(data, { x: "category", y: "value", text: d => d.value, dy: -6 })
  ]
})

tickX

Plot.tickX(data, { x: "value", stroke: "category" }).plot()

tip (tooltip on hover)

Plot.plot({
  marks: [
    Plot.dot(data, { x: "x", y: "y", fill: "series" }),
    Plot.tip(data, Plot.pointer({ x: "x", y: "y", title: d => `${d.label}: ${d.y}` }))
  ]
})

Multi-Y / Faceted

multiY — multiple y series on one chart

Plot.plot({
  marks: [
    Plot.lineY(data, { x: "date", y: "value1", stroke: "steelblue" }),
    Plot.lineY(data, { x: "date", y: "value2", stroke: "orange" })
  ]
})

faceted — small multiples by category

Plot.barY(data, { x: "date", y: "value", fill: "series", fx: "series" }).plot()

Canada Map

provinces — basic outline

const provinces = topojson.feature(topo, topo.objects.provinces);
const provincemesh = topojson.mesh(topo, topo.objects.provinces);
Plot.plot({
  projection: "identity",
  marks: [
    Plot.geo(nation, { fill: "#ddd" }),
    Plot.geo(provincemesh, { stroke: "white" }),
  ]
})

provinces — filled by name with tooltip

Plot.plot({
  projection: "identity",
  marks: [
    Plot.geo(provinces, { fill: d => d.properties.NAME, tip: true,
      title: d => d.properties.NAME }),
    Plot.geo(provincemesh, { stroke: "white", strokeWidth: 0.5 })
  ]
})
